title: Smoky Tender Brazilian Braise
description: A hearty, smoky, and tender Brazilian dish featuring Castelvetrano olives and served in a bamboo steamer. Perfect for a cozy evening with family or friends.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with cumin, smoked paprika, and brown sugar.
Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove from heat and set aside.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot, cooking until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the pitted Castelvetrano olives, Worcestershire sauce, and white wine to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and bring to a boil. Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the pork shoulder for 2 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
While the pork is braising, toast the cubed double-rise bread in the oven until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
To serve, shred the braised pork and mix with the toasted bread and chopped cilantro.
Serve hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
Serve the Smoky Tender Brazilian Braise in a bamboo steamer, if desired.
Adjust the seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with additional Castelvetrano olives and cilantro, if desired.
Serve with your favorite side dishes or enjoy on its own as a hearty, comforting meal.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
